skill.name: telegram-bot-builder
skill.description: Helps design, implement, and operate Telegram bots using common bot libraries, conversation patterns, payments, and scalable architecture.
skill.summary: Design and build practical Telegram bots with architecture, UX, monetization, and operations guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, JavaScript template literals, and ordinary documentation text. The only confirmed concern is example access to bot and payment tokens through environment variables, which is normal for Telegram bot development and has no evidence of exfiltration or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals inside documentation examples. No instruction asks the agent to run shell commands, and no executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are visible in Markdown examples, not Ruby or shell execution. The repository contains only SKILL.md, so there is no executable companion code using these patterns.

low_findings.title: Environment Token Handling Requires Standard Secret Hygiene
low_findings.description: The examples read BOT_TOKEN and PAYMENT_TOKEN from environment variables and show a .env file in a project layout. This is a normal pattern for bot credentials, but users should avoid committing secret files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token references are direct and confirmed, but they are used in ordinary Telegram bot examples. I found no evidence that the skill sends secrets to unauthorized destinations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Blocker Patterns Not Supported by Context
low_findings.description: Static blocker findings for weak cryptography and system reconnaissance do not match the reviewed text. The referenced lines contain descriptive prose, table content, or payment copy, not cryptographic operations or reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found no code or instruction related to weak cryptography, host discovery, or system enumeration. The evidence supports scanner misclassification.

content.value_statement: Telegram bot projects often fail because architecture, interaction design, and operations are planned too late. This skill guides bot design from first command flows through payments, scaling, and user retention.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Telegram bot stacks such as Telegraf, grammY, python-telegram-bot, and aiogram.
Outlines maintainable project structures for commands, handlers, keyboards, middleware, and services.
Designs inline keyboard flows, pagination patterns, and callback handling approaches.
Explains monetization models, Telegram Payments, freemium tiers, and usage limits.
Highlights operational concerns such as webhook management, onboarding, analytics, and graceful errors.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete production bot repository.
It does not verify current Telegram API changes without separate documentation review.
It does not manage bot tokens, payment credentials, hosting, or database configuration.
It does not replace security review for bots that process payments or personal data.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Service Bot
use_cases.description: Plan commands, onboarding, and stack choices for a new Telegram bot before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Bot UX
use_cases.description: Redesign menus, inline keyboards, pagination, and error flows so users can complete common tasks faster.

use_cases.title: Plan Paid Bot Features
use_cases.description: Compare freemium, subscription, per-use, and payment flows for a Telegram bot business model.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Bot Stack
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first Telegram bot
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Telegram bot stack. My bot will do [task], serve [audience], and run on [hosting]. Compare Node.js and Python options.

prompt_templates.title: Design Command Flows
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the command list, onboarding flow, and inline keyboard menus for a Telegram bot that helps users [goal]. Include error states.

prompt_templates.title: Add Payments and Limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a monetization plan for my Telegram bot. Include free limits, premium features, Telegram Payments flow, and abuse prevention.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Telegram bot architecture for scale, reliability, observability, webhook handling, secret management, and user retention risks: [architecture].

output_examples.input: I need a bot for appointment reminders.
output: Recommended stack with reasons for the project size and hosting model.
Command map for start, schedule, cancel, settings, and help flows.
Inline keyboard plan for selecting dates, times, reminder channels, and confirmations.

output_examples.input: My bot has low retention after first use.
output: Onboarding audit focused on first response, menu clarity, and recovery paths.
Suggestions for fewer messages, better notification control, and clearer empty states.
Analytics events to measure activation, repeat use, and drop-off points.

output_examples.input: I want to charge for premium access.
output: Comparison of subscription, per-use, and freemium models.
Payment flow outline using Telegram invoices and post-payment activation.
Usage limit policy that keeps the free tier useful without harming conversion.

best_practices: Store bot and payment tokens in environment variables and keep .env files out of version control.
Acknowledge user actions quickly, then process slow work in the background.
Design clear commands, concise menus, and graceful error messages before adding advanced features.
anti_patterns: Do not send frequent unsolicited messages or force users through long menu chains.
Do not block the bot while waiting for slow APIs, payments, or background jobs.
Do not launch paid features without limits, refund handling, and payment failure paths.

faq.question: Does this skill build a complete Telegram bot?
faq.answer: It provides architecture, examples, and implementation guidance. You still need to create and deploy the bot project.

faq.question: Which Telegram bot libraries does it cover?
faq.answer: It mentions Telegraf, grammY, python-telegram-bot, and aiogram as common options for different project needs.

faq.question: Can it help with Telegram Payments?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers invoice flow, payment tokens, premium activation, and usage limits at a planning level.

faq.question: Does it handle secret management?
faq.answer: It shows environment variable usage, but users must configure secret storage and access controls themselves.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation-only, with no confirmed malicious instructions or executable payloads.

faq.question: Who is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It suits developers, product builders, and teams planning Telegram bots with commands, payments, and user flows.
